content.text: Enough signal: Remote DXB is an aggregator, not an employer ATS — I'll lock a recommendation that fits this app's company-centric model.

input.name: Remote DXB ingest
input.overview: Remote DXB (remotedxb.com) is a UAE remote-job aggregator, not a company ATS. The best fit for this app is a dedicated aggregator importer into a new `uae` country that splits listings by employer name — not the existing single-company `rss` ATS path.
input.plan:
# Add Remote DXB opportunities

## What remotedxb.com actually is

- Curated **UAE/Dubai remote + hybrid job board** (~4k live; sitemap has 16k+ `/job/...` URLs including stale).
- Stack: Laravel + Inertia. **No public jobs API.**
- Useful surfaces:
  - [`https://www.remotedxb.com/rss`](https://www.remotedxb.com/rss) — latest **100** items only; has `title`, `link`, HTML `description`, `category`, plus `remoteDxb:companyName` / logo.
  - Job pages expose rich Inertia `props.listing` (and JSON-LD `JobPosting`): `companyName`, `description`, `remoteStatus`, `applyLink`, etc.
- Apply flow is **through Remote DXB** (`applyMethod: "link"`, `applyLink: https://www.remotedxb.com/apply/...`, UI shows “Sign in to apply”) — not Greenhouse/Lever apply URLs.

So this is closer to “another relocate.me / job board source” than to EVBox’s single-employer RSS.

## How your app ingests jobs today

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  relocateMe[relocate.me country pages] --> buildCompanies[build_companies]
  buildCompanies --> catalog[Postgres company catalog]
  catalog --> atsFetch["fetch_ats_board per company"]
  atsFetch --> jobs[matching_jobs]
```

- Spine is **company → ATS URL → board fetch** ([`relocation_jobs/scrape/board.py`](relocation_jobs/scrape/board.py)): Greenhouse, Lever, Workday, … and a simple [`rss`](relocation_jobs/scrape/boards/http_sync.py) fetcher used for one employer feed (EVBox).
- Board is **per country** (germany / netherlands / uk / portugal + custom countries). UAE is not a first-class country today.
- Manual one-offs: [`add_manual_jobs`](relocation_jobs/companies/service.py) / MCP `add_position`.

**Mismatch:** Remote DXB is many employers under one site. Naively pointing `ats_type=rss` at `/rss` would dump ≤100 jobs under one fake company card and ignore `companyName`.

## Recommendation (best method)

**Build a dedicated Remote DXB aggregator importer** — do **not** reuse generic `ats_type=rss` as the long-term path.

Concrete shape:

1. **Add country `uae`** (label “UAE” / Dubai) via existing custom-country machinery (`add_custom_country` / panel).
2. **New board path** (e.g. `ats_type=remotedxb` *or* a one-shot/scheduled importer script — prefer a real fetcher wired like other boards so panel country fetch can refresh it).
3. **Discovery:** prefer sitemap `/job/...` URLs (full set) over RSS (cap 100). Use RSS only for “what’s new” incremental refresh if useful.
4. **Detail:** parse JobPosting JSON-LD and/or Inertia `listing` for title, description, `companyName`, `remoteStatus`, `applyLink`, posted date.
5. **Catalog mapping:** upsert a **real company row per `companyName`** under `uae`, insert jobs under that company with:
   - `url` = remotedxb job `href` (stable idempotency), and/or store `applyLink` if you want apply to go to their apply endpoint
   - `location` / remote label from `remoteStatus` (e.g. “Completely Remote”)
6. **Filters on ingest** (recommended defaults to avoid flooding the board):
   - category ≈ Information Technology
   - `remoteStatus` ≈ Completely Remote
   - skip `isClosed`
7. **Location matching:** extend UAE/Dubai aliases in [`location_tags.py`](relocation_jobs/core/location_tags.py) if you rely on country filters for this catalog.

### Why this beats the alternatives

| Approach | Verdict |
|----------|---------|
| Generic `rss` company → `/rss` | Fast demo only; 100-job cap; loses employer identity; poor board UX |
| Playwright HTML scrape of listing pages | Fragile; Inertia/JSON-LD already give structured data |
| Treat DXB like Greenhouse for one company | Wrong model — DXB is not an employer ATS |
| Discover employers → scrape real ATS | Best **apply-URL quality**, but different goal (enrich EU/UAE company set), more research per employer, and DXB often doesn’t expose the employer ATS |
| MCP / manual-add only | Best if you only want a handful of roles |

**Default locked for implementation:** dedicated DXB importer + `uae` country + per-employer companies + IT / completely-remote filter.

## Out of scope / product note

Kuchup’s public positioning is EU relocation. A full unfiltered DXB dump (~4k mixed sales/healthcare/etc.) will muddy the board. The IT + completely-remote filter keeps this useful without pretending DXB is Germany/NL.

## Implementation sketch (when you say go)

- Fetcher module under [`relocation_jobs/scrape/boards/`](relocation_jobs/scrape/boards/) (sitemap + detail parse).
- Register in [`board.py`](relocation_jobs/scrape/board.py) + [`ats_constants.py`](relocation_jobs/core/ats_constants.py).
- Seed one catalog entry (or importer that creates companies dynamically from `companyName`).
- Tests with fixtures from RSS / one JobPosting HTML sample (no live network in CI).
- Optional: panel “add company” with careers URL `https://www.remotedxb.com/` and `ats_type=remotedxb` for scheduled refresh.

## Confirm before build

Reply with which outcome you want if the default is wrong:

1. **Bulk UAE board** (plan above) — recommended
2. **Cherry-pick only** — no new fetcher; use manual / MCP `add_position`
3. **DXB as lead gen** — extract company names, then resolve real ATS URLs into existing EU countries

Best daily strategy is a shared aggregator ingest layer with two adapters — RemoteOK via official JSON API (easy), Remote DXB via RSS + sitemap (harder) — upserting per-employer companies under dedicated board countries, refreshed by the existing fetch scheduler.

  - id: remotedxb-adapter
    content: Implement Remote DXB adapter (daily RSS + optional sitemap/detail enrichment)
  - id: countries-schedule
    content: Add board countries (e.g. uae, remote-ok) and wire seed sources into FETCH_SCHEDULE cycle
  - id: filters-tests

isProject: false
---

# Daily ingest — RemoteOK + Remote DXB

## Verdict

| Platform | Best daily strategy | Why |
|----------|---------------------|-----|
| **[RemoteOK](https://remoteok.com)** | Official JSON API (`GET https://remoteok.com/api`) | Free, CORS-open, structured jobs, one HTTP call per refresh |
| **[Remote DXB](https://www.remotedxb.com)** | Dedicated scraper: RSS for daily delta + sitemap/detail for depth | No public jobs API; RSS alone caps at 100 |

**Do not** use the existing single-employer `ats_type=rss` path for either board long-term (loses company identity; RemoteOK’s RSS is gone/`410` anyway).

Both are **aggregators**. Your spine is **company → ATS**. The right fit is a thin **aggregator source** that upserts **one catalog company per employer name**, then stores jobs under those companies — refreshed on the existing EC2 schedule worker.

```mermaid
flowchart TB
  sched[fetch_scheduler_worker every 6h]
  agg[aggregator ingest]
  rok[RemoteOK API adapter]
  dxb[Remote DXB RSS or sitemap adapter]
  catalog[Postgres companies plus jobs]
  board[Panel board by country]

  sched --> agg
  agg --> rok
  agg --> dxb
  rok --> catalog
  dxb --> catalog
  catalog --> board
```

---

## RemoteOK — what the site gives you

- **Public API:** [`https://remoteok.com/api`](https://remoteok.com/api) → JSON array.
  - Element `0` is metadata (`legal`, `last_updated`) — **skip it**.
  - Remaining ~**99** jobs are a rolling window (not the full historic board).
  - Tag filters work, e.g. `?tags=dev` / `?tags=software` (still ~window-sized).
- **Fields per job:** `id`, `slug`, `company`, `position`, `description`, `tags`, `location`, `url`, `apply_url`, `date`/`epoch`, salary min/max, logo.
- **Apply URLs** currently point at `remoteOK.com/remote-jobs/...` (attribution URL), not employer ATS.
- **Legal (must honor):** credit “Remote OK”, link **directly** to each Remote OK job URL (follow, no nofollow). Feed is **~24h delayed** vs the website (instant API is paid/enterprise).
- **RSS:** `remote-jobs.rss` returns **410 Gone** — API only.
- robots.txt allows general crawl but blocks AJAX `action=get_jobs`; irrelevant if you use `/api`.

**Daily method:** one scheduled `GET /api` (optionally with `tags=dev` or a small tag set), map → catalog. Cheapest reliable path in this whole product.

---

## Remote DXB — what the site gives you

- UAE/Dubai remote+hybrid aggregator (~4k live; sitemap ~16k `/job/...` URLs including stale).
- **No public jobs API.**
- **RSS** [`/rss`](https://www.remotedxb.com/rss): latest **100**, with `companyName`, description HTML, category.
- Job pages: Inertia `props.listing` + JSON-LD `JobPosting` (`companyName`, `remoteStatus`, `applyLink`, description, …).
- Apply is **through remotedxb** (`/apply/...`, often “Sign in to apply”).

**Daily method:**

1. **Every schedule cycle:** pull `/rss` → upsert new/changed jobs (cheap, enough for “what’s new today”).
2. **Less often (e.g. weekly) or on demand:** sitemap scan + detail parse for IT / Completely Remote to backfill beyond the RSS window.

---

## Shared strategy that fits *this* app

Your daily machinery already exists: [`relocation_jobs/fetch/scheduler.py`](relocation_jobs/fetch/scheduler.py) + `scripts/fetch_scheduler_worker.py` (`FETCH_SCHEDULE_ENABLED`, default **every 6h**, per-country company fetches).

### Recommended architecture (locked)

1. **Aggregator ingest module** (new), not “pretend Greenhouse”:
   - Input: source key (`remoteok` | `remotedxb`)
   - Output: list of normalized listings `{company, title, url, apply_url?, location?, description?, posted_at?, tags?}`
   - Persist: **upsert company by `(country_key, company_name)`** then `insert_jobs` / board sync (reuse [`catalog/repo.py`](relocation_jobs/catalog/repo.py) upsert helpers)
2. **Board countries** (custom countries):
   - `uae` — Remote DXB listings
   - `remote-ok` (or `worldwide`) — RemoteOK listings  
   Keep them separate from germany/nl/uk/portugal so EU relocation boards stay clean.
3. **Seed one “source” row per aggregator** (or call ingest directly from scheduler) so a country fetch refreshes that source:
   - RemoteOK: `ats_type=remoteok`, `ats_url=https://remoteok.com/api?tags=dev`
   - Remote DXB: `ats_type=remotedxb`, `ats_url=https://www.remotedxb.com/rss` (daily); optional second mode for sitemap
4. **Important catalog twist:** a normal ATS fetch writes jobs under **one** company. Aggregators must **fan out** to many companies inside the fetcher/pipeline (custom post-fetch sync), not `sync_company_board_to_catalog` on a mega-company alone.
5. **Filters (defaults):**
   - RemoteOK: keep jobs whose tags intersect `{dev, engineering, software, devops, backend, frontend, fullstack, …}` (tune later)
   - Remote DXB: category Information Technology + `remoteStatus` Completely Remote; skip closed
6. **URLs / attribution:**
   - RemoteOK: store `url` = Remote OK job page (legal + apply)
   - Remote DXB: store job `href`; optionally also surface `applyLink`
7. **Schedule:** include `uae` and `remote-ok` in `FETCH_SCHEDULE_COUNTRIES` (or always-on alongside supported countries). Interval can stay 6h; API/RSS cost is tiny vs scraping hundreds of ATSs.

### Why not other strategies

| Approach | RemoteOK | Remote DXB |
|----------|----------|------------|
| Generic `ats_type=rss` | RSS dead | Only 100 jobs, one fake company |
| Playwright HTML scrape | Unnecessary (API exists) | Fragile vs Inertia/JSON-LD |
| Discover → real ATS only | Good long-term apply quality, weak for *daily board volume* | Same; DXB rarely exposes employer ATS |
| Manual / MCP only | Fine for handful of roles, not daily | Same |
| Dump into germany/nl | Pollutes EU relocation product | Same |

---

## Daily ops picture

| Cadence | RemoteOK | Remote DXB |
|---------|----------|------------|
| Every 6h (existing worker) | `GET /api?tags=…` → upsert | `GET /rss` → upsert |
| Weekly / manual | Optional (API window is enough) | Sitemap + detail for IT remote backfill |
| Cost / risk | Attribution rules; 24h delay | Rate limits, HTML drift, login-gated apply |

---

## Implementation sketch (when you say go)

- `relocation_jobs/scrape/boards/remoteok.py` — parse API JSON, skip legal row, filter tags, `listing_job(...)`.
- `relocation_jobs/scrape/boards/remotedxb.py` — RSS parser (+ sitemap/detail helpers).
- Aggregator fan-out sync (new service/repo path) that creates missing companies under the source country.
- Register types in [`board.py`](relocation_jobs/scrape/board.py) / [`ats_constants.py`](relocation_jobs/core/ats_constants.py).
- Seed companies + custom countries.
- Fixture-based tests (recorded API/RSS samples); no live network in CI.
- Docs note: RemoteOK attribution requirement; public board should show source / link to original Remote OK URL.

## Product note

Both boards are **worldwide / UAE remote**, not EU visa-relocation. Separate countries keep the main EU boards honest. Unfiltered ingest will flood sales/support/healthcare noise — tag/category filters are part of the design, not optional polish.

content.text:
**RemoteOK is the easy daily win; Remote DXB needs a custom scraper.** Same overall pattern for both.

### RemoteOK
- Official feed: `https://remoteok.com/api` (JSON, no auth). Skip element 0 (legal blurb); ~100 rolling jobs.
- Job fields include `company`, `position`, `description`, `tags`, `url` / `apply_url`.
- Must credit Remote OK and link directly to their job URL; feed is ~24h delayed.
- RSS is gone (`410`). **Daily strategy: scheduled `GET /api` (e.g. `?tags=dev`) — one HTTP call.**

### Remote DXB
- No jobs API. RSS = latest 100; full set needs sitemap + job-page parse.
- Apply goes through remotedxb, often behind sign-in.
- **Daily strategy: RSS every cycle; optional weekly sitemap backfill.**

### Best fit for *your* app
Neither is a Greenhouse-style company ATS. Don’t jam them into generic `rss` as one mega-company.

**Shared aggregator ingest:**
1. Adapters: `remoteok` (API) + `remotedxb` (RSS/sitemap)
2. Upsert **one catalog company per employer name**
3. Separate board countries (`remote-ok`, `uae`) so EU boards stay clean
4. Hook into the existing `fetch_scheduler_worker` (already ~every 6h)

Filter on ingest (dev/IT / completely remote) or you’ll flood the board with sales/support noise.

Full comparison and implementation sketch are in the updated plan. Say if you want bulk boards as planned, cherry-pick only, or DXB/RemoteOK as lead-gen into real ATSs — then we can implement.

from __future__ import annotations

import html
import re
from urllib.parse import parse_qs, urlparse

from relocation_jobs.core.ats_detection import HEADERS
from relocation_jobs.scrape.listing import listing_job

_TAG_RE = re.compile(r"<[^>]+>")

DEFAULT_REMOTEOK_API = "https://remoteok.com/api?tags=dev"

_DEV_TAG_HINTS = frozenset({
    "dev",
    "engineering",
    "software",
    "engineer",
    "backend",
    "frontend",
    "fullstack",
    "full-stack",
    "devops",
    "sre",
    "golang",
    "python",
    "java",
    "javascript",
    "typescript",
    "kotlin",
    "rust",
    "api",
    "infra",
    "infrastructure",
    "platform",
})


def remoteok_api_url(board_url: str) -> str:
    raw = (board_url or "").strip() or DEFAULT_REMOTEOK_API
    parsed = urlparse(raw)
    if not parsed.scheme:
        return DEFAULT_REMOTEOK_API
    path = (parsed.path or "").rstrip("/")
    if path in ("", "/api"):
        query = parsed.query
        if query:
            return f"https://remoteok.com/api?{query}"
        return "https://remoteok.com/api"
    if "remoteok.com" in (parsed.netloc or "").lower():
        return raw
    return DEFAULT_REMOTEOK_API


def remoteok_tag_filters(board_url: str) -> frozenset[str]:
    parsed = urlparse(remoteok_api_url(board_url))
    qs = parse_qs(parsed.query)
    tags: set[str] = set()
    for key in ("tag", "tags"):
        for raw in qs.get(key) or []:
            for part in str(raw).split(","):
                tag = part.strip().lower()
                if tag:
                    tags.add(tag)
    return frozenset(tags)


def _plain_text(raw: str) -> str:
    text = html.unescape(raw or "")
    text = _TAG_RE.sub(" ", text)
    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", text).strip()


def _tags_match(job_tags: list, wanted: frozenset[str]) -> bool:
    if not wanted:
        return True
    have = {str(tag).strip().lower() for tag in job_tags if tag}
    if have & wanted:
        return True
    if wanted <= _DEV_TAG_HINTS and have & _DEV_TAG_HINTS:
        return True
    return False


def parse_remoteok_api_payload(payload: object, *, board_url: str = "") -> list[dict]:
    if not isinstance(payload, list):
        return []
    wanted = remoteok_tag_filters(board_url)
    jobs: list[dict] = []
    for row in payload:
        if not isinstance(row, dict):
            continue
        if "legal" in row or "last_updated" in row and "position" not in row:
            continue
        title = (row.get("position") or row.get("title") or "").strip()
        url = (row.get("url") or row.get("apply_url") or "").strip()
        employer = (row.get("company") or "").strip()
        if not title or not url or not employer:
            continue
        tags = row.get("tags") if isinstance(row.get("tags"), list) else []
        if not _tags_match(tags, wanted):
            continue
        location = (row.get("location") or "").strip().rstrip(",")
        description = _plain_text(row.get("description") or "")
        jobs.append(
            listing_job(
                title,
                url,
                location=location or None,
                employer=employer,
                description_text=description or None,
            )
        )
    return jobs


async def fetch_remoteok_board(client, board_url: str, company: dict) -> list[dict]:
    url = remoteok_api_url(board_url or (company.get("ats_url") or company.get("careers_url") or ""))
    response = await client.get(url, headers=HEADERS, timeout=30.0)
    response.raise_for_status()
    return parse_remoteok_api_payload(response.json(), board_url=url)

def _upsert_jobs_additive_on_conn(conn, company_id: int, jobs: list[dict]) -> int:
    touched = 0
    for job in jobs:
        stamp_job_identity(job)
        key = job.get("idempotency_key") or job_idempotency_key(job.get("url", ""))
        if not key:
            continue
        conn.execute(
            """
            INSERT INTO matching_jobs (
                company_id, idempotency_key, title, url, fetched, last_seen,
                visa_sponsorship, location, locations_json, description_text
            ) VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)
            ON CONFLICT (company_id, idempotency_key) DO UPDATE SET
                title = EXCLUDED.title,
                url = EXCLUDED.url,
                last_seen = EXCLUDED.last_seen,
                visa_sponsorship = EXCLUDED.visa_sponsorship,
                location = COALESCE(NULLIF(EXCLUDED.location, ''), matching_jobs.location),
                locations_json = CASE
                    WHEN EXCLUDED.locations_json IS NOT NULL
                         AND EXCLUDED.locations_json != '[]'
                    THEN EXCLUDED.locations_json
                    ELSE matching_jobs.locations_json
                END,
                description_text = CASE
                    WHEN EXCLUDED.description_text IS NOT NULL
                         AND EXCLUDED.description_text != ''
                    THEN EXCLUDED.description_text
                    ELSE matching_jobs.description_text
                END
            """,
            (
                company_id,
                key,
                job.get("title") or "",
                job.get("url") or "",
                job.get("fetched") or _today_iso(),
                job.get("last_seen") or job.get("fetched") or _today_iso(),
                _visa_to_db(job.get("visa_sponsorship")),
                (job.get("location") or "").strip(),
                job_locations_json(job),
                (job.get("description_text") or "").strip(),
            ),
        )
        touched += 1
    return touched


def sync_aggregator_employer_jobs(
    country_key: str,
    employer_name: str,
    jobs: list[dict],
    *,
    source: str,
    careers_url: str,
) -> int:
    name = (employer_name or "").strip()
    if not name or not jobs:
        return 0
    ts = _today_iso()
    source_key = (source or "aggregator").strip().lower() or "aggregator"
    company = {
        "name": name,
        "city": "",
        "size": "",
        "careers_url": (careers_url or "").strip(),
        "ats_type": "sourced",
        "ats_url": "",
        "matching_jobs": [],
        "sources": [source_key],
        "added": ts,
        "updated": ts,
        "fetch_ok": True,
        "fetch_ok_date": ts,
    }
    with db_transaction() as conn:
        company_id = _upsert_company_row_on_conn(conn, country_key, company, updated=ts)
        touched = _upsert_jobs_additive_on_conn(conn, company_id, jobs)
        count_row = conn.execute(
            "SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM companies WHERE country = %s",
            (country_key,),
        ).fetchone()
        total = int(_row(count_row).get("n") or 0)
        _ensure_country_meta(conn, country_key, updated=ts, total=total)
    if touched:
        invalidate_country_cache(country_key)
    return touched

) -> tuple[str, int]:
    name = company.get("name") or ""
    raw = await fetch_board(client, company)
    log_event(f"board returned {len(raw)} raw job(s)", company=name)
    raise_if_cancelled()
    employers, job_total = sync_aggregator_board(
        catalog_country,
        company,
        raw,
        relevant_only=True,
    )
    if review_mode and on_review:
        matched = filter_relevant_jobs(raw, True)
        on_review(build_review_payload(included=matched, filtered=[]))
    if on_company_result and job_total > 0:
        on_company_result(name, job_total, _slim_new_jobs(filter_relevant_jobs(raw, True)))
    company["matching_jobs"] = []
    company["updated"] = now_iso()
    _mark_fetch_ok(company)
    _call_sync_board(sync_board)
    return aggregator_success_line(prefix, employers, job_total), job_total

Shipped. Daily aggregator ingest for **Remote OK** and **Remote DXB** is wired into the existing fetch path.

### What you get
- **Remote OK** → `GET /api?tags=dev` (official JSON; attribution URLs kept)
- **Remote DXB** → RSS (IT category only)
- Fan-out: each listing’s employer becomes its own catalog company (`ats_type=sourced`, skipped by country fetch)
- Seeds on panel boot + scheduler boot:
  - country `remote-ok` / company **Remote OK**
  - country `uae` / company **Remote DXB**

### How to refresh
1. Restart panel (or start the fetch worker) so seeds exist  
2. Fetch **Remote OK** / **Remote DXB** from the panel, or include `remote-ok,uae` in `FETCH_SCHEDULE_COUNTRIES`  
3. Browse those countries on the board — jobs sit under employer companies

### Key files
- [`relocation_jobs/scrape/boards/remoteok.py`](relocation_jobs/scrape/boards/remoteok.py)
- [`relocation_jobs/scrape/boards/remotedxb.py`](relocation_jobs/scrape/boards/remotedxb.py)

- [`relocation_jobs/scrape/aggregator_seeds.py`](relocation_jobs/scrape/aggregator_seeds.py)

content.text:
Yes — the pipeline is working. The low count is from the **title relevance filter**, not a fan-out bug.

Right now a live pull looks like this:

| Source | After adapter | After title filter | Employers kept |
|--------|---------------|--------------------|----------------|
| RemoteOK | ~86 | **1** | 1 |
| Remote DXB (IT RSS) | ~13 | **1** | 1 |

Almost everything is dropped by the same rules as EU ATS scrapes: must match narrow include keywords (`software engineer`, `backend`, `platform engineer`, …) and must not hit excludes (`principal`, `junior`, `staff`, `devops`, `security`, sales, etc.).

So roles like “Principal Software Engineer”, “Staff Engineer”, “DevOps Engineer”, “React Native Engineer” are intentionally rejected.

**Bottom line:** one company / one job per board today is expected with the current filter — these aggregators are noisy, and the filter is tuned for your backend-focused board.

If you want more volume from RemoteOK/DXB, we can loosen the filter **only for aggregators** (e.g. keep any `* Engineer` / `* Developer`, stop excluding principal/staff/devops). Say if you want that.

) -> tuple[str, int]:
    name = company.get("name") or ""

        relevant_only=True,
    )

        filtered_out = review_filtered_jobs(

        log_event(
            f"review: {len(matched)} included, {len(filtered_out)} filtered",
            company=name,
        )
    if on_company_result and job_total > 0:
        on_company_result(name, job_total, _slim_new_jobs(matched))
    company["matching_jobs"] = []
    company["updated"] = now_iso()
    _mark_fetch_ok(company)
    _call_sync_board(sync_board)
